model_narrative: We operate a single hydro reservoir together with hard-coal and nuclear plants to meet electricity demand across every hour of a full year. In each hour we decide how much each technology generates, how much water is held in the reservoir and how much is spilled, how much expensive gap generation is needed to cover any shortfall, and how far the reservoir level is allowed to stray beyond its permitted band. Hydro generation and spillage draw down the reservoir while weekly inflow refills it. The objective is to minimize the total cost over the whole year, made up of the generation cost of each technology plus penalties for gap generation and for any reservoir-level violations.

sets.name: t
sets.doc: the hours of the planning year in chronological order, labelled t1, t2, and so on; the first member is the opening hour and each later member follows the one before it, and the very last hour is the terminal hour of the horizon

sets.name: w
sets.doc: the weeks of the planning year in chronological order; each hour belongs to exactly one week, and a fixed number of consecutive hours make up each week

sets.name: ft
sets.doc: the generation technologies available, namely a hydro plant fed by the reservoir, a hard-coal plant, and a nuclear plant

sets.name: pc
sets.doc: the price columns carrying the weekly coal and CO2 prices used to derive hard-coal generation cost

params.name: demand
params.doc: the electricity demand to be met in each hour, in megawatts

params.name: exchange
params.doc: the net cross-border power flow available in each hour, in megawatts, which offsets part of the demand that domestic generation must cover

params.name: wInflow
params.doc: the total water inflow into the reservoir over each week, expressed in megawatt-hours per week; the per-hour inflow in any hour is this weekly figure divided by the number of hours in a week

params.name: wCapacity
params.doc: the maximum generation each technology can produce, in megawatts, given for each week and technology; the capacity applying in an hour is the value for that hour's week

params.name: wPrices
params.doc: the weekly hard-coal and CO2 prices in euros per ton, used only to compute generation cost in the objective

params.name: hpw
params.doc: the number of hours in a week, used to split weekly inflow into a per-hour rate and to map each hour to its week

params.name: gencost_nuclear
params.doc: the generating cost of the nuclear plant, in euros per megawatt

params.name: gencost_co2_factor
params.doc: the CO2 emission factor applied to hard-coal generation when building its cost

params.name: gencost_denom
params.doc: the hard-coal efficiency denominator that scales the combined coal-and-CO2 price into a per-megawatt cost

params.name: resmax_normal
params.doc: the maximum allowed reservoir level in any ordinary hour, in megawatts

params.name: resmax_last
params.doc: the maximum allowed reservoir level in the terminal hour of the horizon, in megawatts

params.name: resmin_normal
params.doc: the minimum required reservoir level in any ordinary hour, in megawatts

params.name: resmin_last
params.doc: the minimum required reservoir level in the terminal hour of the horizon, in megawatts

params.name: gap_penalty
params.doc: the penalty charged per megawatt of unserved demand covered by gap generation

params.name: slack_penalty
params.doc: the penalty charged per megawatt of violation of the reservoir level limits

vars.name: GAP
vars.doc: the unserved demand made up by costly gap generation in each hour, in megawatts

vars.name: RES
vars.doc: the reservoir level at the end of each hour, in megawatts

vars.name: SPILL
vars.doc: the water spilled from the reservoir in each hour, in megawatts

vars.name: X
vars.doc: the generation produced by each technology in each hour, in megawatts; the hydro entry draws on the reservoir

vars.name: SLACKUP
vars.doc: the amount by which the reservoir level is allowed to exceed its upper limit in each hour, penalized in the objective

vars.name: SLACKLO
vars.doc: the amount by which the reservoir level is allowed to fall short of its lower limit in each hour, penalized in the objective

description: The reservoir level has to follow a running water balance from one hour to the next. For each hour, the level at the end of that hour equals the level at the end of the previous hour, plus the inflow arriving that hour, minus the water used for hydro generation and minus whatever is spilled. The horizon wraps around, so the hour before the very first hour is the very last hour of the year. The inflow arriving in an hour is that hour's weekly inflow shared evenly across the hours of the week.
expected_pyomo:
def Cont_rule(model, tt):
    t_list = list(model.t)
    w_list = list(model.w)
    idx = t_list.index(tt)
    prev = t_list[idx - 1]
    hpw = len(t_list) // len(w_list)
    week = w_list[idx // hpw]
    return model.RES[tt] - model.RES[prev] + model.X[tt, 'Hydro'] + model.SPILL[tt] == model.wInflow[week] / hpw
model.Cont = Constraint(model.t, rule=Cont_rule)

The objective is to minimize the total cost over the whole year, made up of the generation cost of each technology plus penalties for gap generation and for any reservoir-level violations.

description: Demand must be met in every hour. For each hour, the total generation from all technologies together with any gap generation has to be at least as large as that hour's demand after the available cross-border exchange has been netted off.

description: The reservoir is not allowed to rise above its upper level limit, except that exceedance can be absorbed by a penalized upper slack. For each hour, the reservoir level reduced by that slack must stay at or below the upper limit for that hour, where the final hour of the year uses its own terminal upper limit rather than the ordinary one.
expected_pyomo:
def ResUp_rule(model, tt):
    t_list = list(model.t)
    rmax = model.resmax_last if tt == t_list[-1] else model.resmax_normal
    return -model.RES[tt] + model.SLACKUP[tt] >= -rmax
model.ResUp = Constraint(model.t, rule=ResUp_rule)

description: No technology can generate more than its available capacity. For each hour and each technology, the generation must not exceed that technology's capacity for the week that hour falls in.
expected_pyomo:
def PlantCap_rule(model, tt, ft):
    t_list = list(model.t)
    w_list = list(model.w)
    hpw = len(t_list) // len(w_list)
    week = w_list[t_list.index(tt) // hpw]
    return model.X[tt, ft] <= model.wCapacity[week, ft]
model.PlantCap = Constraint(model.t, model.ft, rule=PlantCap_rule)

description: To build the complete model, enforce the following relationships in order. First, keep a running water balance on the reservoir so that each hour's ending level reflects the previous hour's level plus that hour's share of weekly inflow, less the water used for hydro generation and any spillage, with the horizon wrapping so the hour before the first is the last. Second, make sure demand is met in every hour, with total generation across all technologies plus gap generation covering demand after cross-border exchange is netted off. Third, hold the reservoir at or below its upper level limit in each hour, allowing a penalized upper slack to absorb any exceedance and using a separate terminal limit in the final hour. Fourth, hold the reservoir at or above its lower level limit in each hour, allowing a penalized lower slack to cover any shortfall and using a separate terminal limit in the final hour. Finally, cap the generation of each technology in each hour at that technology's capacity for the week the hour belongs to.
expected_pyomo:
def Cont_rule(model, tt):
    t_list = list(model.t)
    w_list = list(model.w)
    idx = t_list.index(tt)
    prev = t_list[idx - 1]
    hpw = len(t_list) // len(w_list)
    week = w_list[idx // hpw]
    return model.RES[tt] - model.RES[prev] + model.X[tt, 'Hydro'] + model.SPILL[tt] == model.wInflow[week] / hpw
model.Cont = Constraint(model.t, rule=Cont_rule)
def DemSat_rule(model, tt):
    return sum(model.X[tt, ft] for ft in model.ft) + model.GAP[tt] >= model.demand[tt] - model.exchange[tt]
model.DemSat = Constraint(model.t, rule=DemSat_rule)
def ResUp_rule(model, tt):
    t_list = list(model.t)
    rmax = model.resmax_last if tt == t_list[-1] else model.resmax_normal
    return -model.RES[tt] + model.SLACKUP[tt] >= -rmax
model.ResUp = Constraint(model.t, rule=ResUp_rule)
def ResLo_rule(model, tt):
    t_list = list(model.t)
    rmin = model.resmin_last if tt == t_list[-1] else model.resmin_normal
    return model.RES[tt] + model.SLACKLO[tt] >= rmin
model.ResLo = Constraint(model.t, rule=ResLo_rule)
def PlantCap_rule(model, tt, ft):
    t_list = list(model.t)
    w_list = list(model.w)
    hpw = len(t_list) // len(w_list)
    week = w_list[t_list.index(tt) // hpw]
    return model.X[tt, ft] <= model.wCapacity[week, ft]
model.PlantCap = Constraint(model.t, model.ft, rule=PlantCap_rule)
